title: ISO 20022 Message Standard Overview
content:
ISO 20022 is the global financial messaging standard for electronic data interchange between financial institutions. Key message families:
- pacs (Payments Clearing and Settlement): pacs.008 FI-to-FI credit transfer, pacs.009 financial institution credit transfer, pacs.002 payment status report
- camt (Cash Management): camt.053 bank-to-customer statement, camt.054 debit/credit notification
- pain (Payment Initiation): pain.001 customer credit transfer initiation, pain.002 customer payment status report
MX messages use XML with strict namespace declarations. Each message has a Business Application Header (BAH) and a Document element. The namespace URI encodes the message type and version, e.g. urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pacs.008.001.08.

title: NACHA ACH File Format
content:
NACHA governs the ACH network in the US. ACH files use fixed-width 94-character records.
Record types:
- Type 1: File Header — routing transit number, file creation date
- Type 5: Batch Header — company name, company ID, SEC code (PPD/CCD/CTX/WEB), effective date
- Type 6: Entry Detail — transaction code, routing+account, amount (10-digit zero-padded cents), individual name, trace number
- Type 8: Batch Control — entry count, debit/credit totals
- Type 9: File Control — batch count, block count, entry count
Routing numbers: 9 digits. ABA checksum: (3*d[0]+7*d[1]+d[2]+3*d[3]+7*d[4]+d[5]+3*d[6]+7*d[7]+d[8]) mod 10 == 0.
Transaction codes: 22=checking credit, 27=checking debit, 32=savings credit, 37=savings debit.

title: SWIFT MX Migration Timeline
content:
SWIFT is migrating from MT (message text) to MX (ISO 20022 XML) format.
Key dates:
- November 2022: coexistence period began
- November 2025: MT to MX migration deadline for correspondent banking
- November 2026: Hybrid address format mandatory — TwnNm and Ctry must be structured fields; max 2 unstructured AdrLine elements allowed
The pacs.008 replaces MT103. pacs.009 replaces MT202. camt.053 replaces MT940/MT942.
During coexistence the SWIFT translation service converts between MT and MX.

title: ISO 20022 Hybrid Address Format (PostalAddress24)
content:
ISO 20022 postal addresses (PostalAddress24) mix structured and unstructured fields.
Mandatory from November 2026:
- TwnNm (town name): required, max 35 chars
- Ctry (country): required, ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (2 uppercase letters)
Optional structured: StrtNm, BldgNb, PstCd
Optional unstructured: AdrLine[0..2] — max 70 chars each, max 2 lines
Hybrid = TwnNm + Ctry + AdrLine. Full structured = all structured fields, no AdrLine.

title: Cross-Rail Payment Reconciliation Patterns
content:
Reconciliation matches transactions across payment rails.
Key patterns:
- Reference matching: end-to-end ID, UETR, or instruction ID
- Amount tolerance: typical 0.01% for FX rounding
- Status flow: PDNG → ACCP → ACSC → RJCT
- Exception handling: surface unmatched, flag partial, detect duplicates
UETR: 36-char UUID used in SWIFT MX for end-to-end tracking.
Settlement cutoffs: FedNow 24/7, ACH next-day/same-day windows.

title: Payment Data Compliance and PII Requirements
content:
Payment messages contain sensitive PII subject to regulatory requirements.
- GDPR Article 4: name, IBAN, account number are personal data
- FinCEN/BSA: customer due diligence records retained 5 years
- PCI DSS: PAN must be masked to last 4 digits
- Audit trail: every access to account number, IBAN, or amount must be logged
Masking rules: account numbers → last 4 visible (****1234); IBANs → country + last 4.
AML: transactions >$10 000 trigger CTR filing; structuring patterns flagged.

title: Ledger Entry Standard Fields
content:
A LedgerEntry represents one transaction in an internal ledger.
Standard fields:
- entry_id: unique identifier (payment end-to-end ID or reference)
- amount: Decimal; positive = credit, negative = debit
- currency: ISO 4217 3-letter code (USD, EUR, GBP)
- counterparty: name or BIC of the other party
- reference: matching key (UETR, end-to-end ID)
- rail: iso20022 | ach | fedwire | sepa | internal
- value_date: when funds are available
- booking_date: when transaction is booked in the ledger
